title: Machine-Learning-Guided Test Pattern Generation System
abstract: A system and method for generating test patterns in which a learned model orders decision points during a structural search, reducing pattern count and runtime while preserving fault coverage guarantees.

title: Hardware Trojan Detection Using Existing Scan Infrastructure
abstract: A method for detecting malicious hardware modifications by extracting side-channel and structural signatures from pre-existing scan and compression circuitry, avoiding dedicated detection hardware.

title: Reconfigurable Dataflow Mapping for Sparse Tensor Computation
abstract: An accelerator datapath and mapping method that dynamically reconfigures to skip zero-valued operations under structured sparsity, improving energy efficiency for inference workloads.

title: Activity-Aware Clock Mesh Synthesis Method
abstract: A clock-network synthesis method that shapes a clock mesh according to predicted switching activity to reduce dynamic power while meeting skew constraints.

title: Hierarchical Scan Compression Architecture
abstract: A scan-compression architecture organized hierarchically so that test-data volume and application time remain bounded as gate count scales, while preserving diagnosis resolution.

title: Testable Die-to-Die Interconnect for Chiplet Packages
abstract: A die-to-die interconnect with embedded test access that enables known-good-die verification of assembled multi-die packages without dedicated external test structures.
